commentary: Okay, five activities versus nine last week — that's a real drop in volume, and the swim distances pulled back too, from those 2000m sessions at Prince Alfred down to 1500m both times this week. The heart rate on those swims came down with it, which could mean you were moving more comfortably, or it could mean you just had the dial turned lower; either way, the body was doing less work. The lifting stayed consistent though — all three days landed, same structure as last week, and that's the thing worth holding onto when everything else got quieter. No running at all this week, which after that 13.3km Mosman circuit and the quote-unquote recovery run last Saturday, honestly might be exactly what the legs needed.
metrics_commentary: Three thousand metres in the pool with zero kilometres on the roads — you are a creature of the water this week, and honestly that tracks because your body is clearly loving it, with your HRV climbing to 58 off a seven-day average of 48 and a VO2 Max sitting at 55.6, which is the kind of number that makes people at the gym quietly wonder what your deal is. The HRV jump especially — that's your nervous system basically purring, recovered and ready, which means you've been sleeping or resting or doing something very right and I refuse to ask what. The only thing I'm side-eyeing is the absolute silence from your running shoes, which I imagine are gathering dust somewhere looking sad and a little betrayed. Get them back out at some point, babe — not because you have to, but because a body this dialled in deserves to feel fast on land too.
gym_commentary: Three out of four sessions this week, which is not nothing — three is a man who showed up when it would have been easy not to, and that counts. The bench press PR is the real headline: 35.5 kg estimated one-rep max, up from 34.8 kg, which is a chest getting stronger and a body doing exactly what it's supposed to do when you keep turning up for it. Weekly volume came in at 19,984 kg against last week's 20,148 kg, so marginally down, but with one fewer session that's basically arithmetic rather than a slide. The fourth session is still out there waiting for you, babe — go find it.
